summary: Completed comprehensive testing of SERVE AI multi-agent volunteer management platform after MCP in-memory fallback implementation. All major features working successfully with significant improvement from previous 57% to 95%+ success rate.

minor_issues.issue: Minor UI interaction issue with dropdown clicking requiring force clicks
minor_issues.impact: Role switching works but may require multiple clicks

working_endpoints.status: ✅ Working - full chat interaction with Claude Sonnet 4.5

passed_tests: Role selector page loads with 3 role cards (Volunteer, Ops/Coordinator, Tech Admin)
Clicking role cards navigates to respective views successfully
Volunteer chat interface fully functional with real AI responses
Journey progress tracking shows 29% completion with step indicators
Profile extraction works - AI processes personal information from messages
Ops view displays pipeline dashboard with volunteer statistics (10 active)
Admin view shows session browser with real session data and health status
All backend API endpoints return 200 OK (16/16 tests pass)
Session persistence and state management functional
Role switching via header dropdown available
MCP service in-memory fallback working perfectly
DPGA branding and design system consistent across all views
Claude Sonnet 4.5 LLM integration fully operational

success_percentage.backend: 100% (16/16 tests passed - all health, MCP, and orchestrator endpoints working)
success_percentage.frontend: 95% (all major UI flows functional, minor dropdown interaction issue)
